mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household disposable income and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks and inventory imbalances that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while HOUST falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the combined Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, persistently low federal funds rates combined with rising real GDP growth create a favorable liquidity environment that sustains equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistical difference between low-rate/high-GDP regimes and baseline periods (p > 0.10), or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales directly reflects expanding consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between RSAFS momentum and SPX 5-day returns falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes (partial correlation < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls in the same month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in more than 40% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A peak in industrial production followed by a plateau indicates manufacturing capacity saturation and weakening order books, which precedes corporate earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the rolling 3-month correlation between INDPRO deceleration and SPX 5-day returns remains above 0.05.
